description: Swap meteg=siluq (סֽ) and etnachta (ס֑).
refuni: lamed sheva shin sin-dot holam nun sheva alef qamats etnachta meteg yod sof-pasuq
changeuni: lamed sheva shin sin-dot holam nun sheva alef qamats meteg etnachta yod sof-pasuq
notes: This verse matches Deut 5:9.

The meteg (05bd) is the Lower accent; the etnachta (0591) is the Upper accent. In the LC the Lower accent, meteg, is placed before (right-to-left) the Upper accent, etnachta. The reference Unicode text places the Upper accent, etnachta, first.
The LC and text results appear to agree. Changing the mark order in the Unicode text reflects actual LC mark ordering and has no effect on the displayed text.
Recommended.

The revia and gereshayim have the same combining group (11) and can be placed in any order without "breaking" the mark ordering of the text.

description: Swap merkha (ס֥) and mahapakh (ס֤).

In changes 1-3, 5-8, the first accent in the LC is the lower accent, which in this case is the merkha. The reference text places the upper accent, mahapakh, first.

The merka and the mahapakh have the same combining group (7) and can be placed in any order without "breaking" the mark ordering of the text. The SBL Hebrew font doesn't display the changed ordering correctly. The reference ordering appears in Deut 5:10.1;whereas the changed ordering appears in Ex 20:6.1.
Recommended.

description: Replace holam with revia, add note 't'.
refuni: alef hataf-patah zero-width-joiner meteg dalet holam nun qamats yod
changeuni: alef hataf-patah zero-width-joiner meteg dalet nun qamats revia yod
notes: The LC has a dot above the nun. The transcription problem is to determine whether the dot is a holam or a revia. If the dot represents a holam, then the word is without an accent; if it represents a revia it lacks an expected vowel.

Seth (Avi) Kadish points out: "Many masoretic manuscripts leave out the ḥolam dot in the divine name as a rule, and some do so even when it is spelled אדני. While the LC itself normally has the ḥolam dot in the spelling אדני, here it does not. This should be considered an anomaly rather than an error, and the dot that appears directly over the letter נ should be understood as a revia` rather than ḥolam. It is even possible that the single dot is intended to serve both purposes."

description: Add maqaf, change tipeha to meteg, add note 't'.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva final-kaf qamats tipeha
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva final-kaf qamats meteg maqaf
notes: The maqaf is clear in the LC.

The accent under the qamats of the final kaf could be either a tipeha, a primary accent, or a meteg, a secondary accent. A tipeha is a diagonal line from northwest to southeast, a meteg is a vertical line. When a maqaf is added to this word it becomes a poetic word with the following word, Ps 31:2.2. Poetic words normally contain a single primary accent. Word Ps 31:2.2 has a clear primary munah accent, thus the accent in question is likely to be a meteg, a secondary accent.
Dotan (Biblia Hebraica Leningradensia, 2001) has a meteg for the accent in question.

description: Change pashta on final-kaf to qadma.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva shin shin-dot patah lamed dagesh tsere meteg het hataf-patah final-kaf qamats pashta
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva shin shin-dot patah lamed dagesh tsere meteg het hataf-patah final-kaf qamats qadma
notes: A qadma better represents the mark on the final-kaf .

description: Change accent on nun to tipeha; add note 't'.
refuni: mem hiriq lamed dagesh hiriq pe sheva nun tsere merkha yod
changeuni: mem hiriq lamed dagesh hiriq pe sheva nun tsere tipeha yod
notes: The accent on the nun is probably a tipeha.
The next change, Deut 17:18.15, is compatible with this change.

description: Change accent on nun to merkha; add note 't'.
refuni: he patah kaf dagesh holam he hataf-patah nun hiriq tipeha yod final-mem
changeuni: he patah kaf dagesh holam he hataf-patah nun hiriq merkha yod final-mem
notes: The accent on the nun is probably a merkha.
The previous change, Deut 17:18.14, is compatible with this change.
This additional change was suggested by Benjamin Denckla.

description: Change accent on shin from geresh to revia; add note 't'.
refuni: tav dagesh patah ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot segol geresh he
changeuni: tav dagesh patah ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot segol revia he
notes: The text is of very poor quality.
The change of the geresh to a revia was suggested by Benjamin Denckla.

description: Remove qadma from kaf.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva kaf qamats qadma lamed maqaf
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva kaf qamats qadma lamed maqaf
changeuni_gen: bet dagesh sheva kaf qamats lamed maqaf

description: Add maqaf; add note 'c'.
refuni: vav sheva resh qamats mahapakh bet sheva tsadi qamats he
changeuni: vav sheva resh qamats mahapakh bet sheva tsadi qamats he maqaf
notes: The maqaf is unexpected as this word and the following word both have accents.
The presence of a maqaf here in the LC is disputed among scholars.

description: Change merkha on dalet to a meteg. Add 'm' note.
refuni: pe dagesh patah dalet dagesh segol merkha nun qamats meteg he
changeuni: pe dagesh patah dalet dagesh segol meteg nun qamats meteg he
notes: The near-vertical line associated with the dalet lacks a northeast-southwest inclination. The near-vertical line associated with the nun lacks a northeast-southwest inclination. The UXLC text will be changed to have a meteg under the dalet to correspond to its appearance in the Leningrad Codex.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha under the dalet and meteg under the nun.

description: Change meteg under het to merkha. Add note 't'.
refuni: het qamats meteg tet sheva alef tevir vav dagesh
changeuni: het qamats merkha tet sheva alef tevir vav dagesh
notes: The near-vertical line under the het has a slight northeast-southwest inclination.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the het.

description: Add maqaf between the first he and the zayin.
refuni: lamed qamats merkha mem dagesh qamats he space zayin dagesh segol tipeha he
changeuni: lamed qamats merkha mem dagesh qamats he maqaf zayin dagesh segol tipeha he
notes: The putative maqaf is very faint. A maqaf at this point would place two accents on a single poetic word.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) does not have a maqaf between these words.

description: Change meteg under nun to merkha. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva nun qamats meteg tav sheva nun tevir vav dagesh
changeuni: vav sheva nun qamats merkha tav sheva nun tevir vav dagesh
notes: The near-vertical line on the nun has a very slight northeast-southwest inclination.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the nun.

description: Replace meteg under nun with munah.
refuni: vav dagesh bet sheva nun tsere meteg yod
changeuni: vav dagesh bet sheva nun tsere munah yod

description: Place pashta above sin, replace merkha with meteg below. Add note 't'.
refuni: tav patah ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot segol merkha he maqaf
changeuni: tav patah ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot segol pashta meteg he maqaf
notes: This text is in a Decalogue and is expected to have dual accents.
The mark above the sin is small and light, starting on the rightmost leg of the sin. The near-vertical line below the shin is faint, short and broken, more likely to be a meteg than merkha.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a pashta over the shin and a meteg below.

description: Interchange order of tevir and munah on mem so that the tevir comes first.
refuni: shin shin-dot qamats mem munah tevir vav holam resh
changeuni: shin shin-dot qamats mem tevir munah vav holam resh
notes: This text is in a Decalogue and is expected to have dual accents.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has tevir then the munah on the mem.

description: Change meteg under tsadi to tipeha.
refuni: tav dagesh rafe hiriq resh sheva tsadi qamats meteg het sof-pasuq
changeuni: tav dagesh rafe hiriq resh sheva tsadi qamats tipeha het sof-pasuq
notes: This text is in a Decalogue and is expected to have dual accents.
Only one accent is below the tsadi and it's curved like a tipeha.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has tipeha then meteg under the tsadi.

description: Change meteg to merkha under yod.
refuni: yod qamats meteg dalet sheva final-kaf qamats tevir
changeuni: yod qamats merkha dalet sheva final-kaf qamats tevir
notes: The near-vertical line under the yod moves distinctly from northeast to southwest. This is the best example of a merkha in these changes.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the yod.

description: Change meteg to merkha under yod. Add 't' note.
refuni: yod holam meteg shin shin-dot sheva bet tsere tevir yod
changeuni: yod holam merkha shin shin-dot sheva bet tsere tevir yod
notes: The near-vertical line under the yod moves slightly from northeast to southwest. Note that the line appears to be made two overwritten lines, one of which is vertical.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the yod.

description: Replace meteg under pe with merkha. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav dagesh pe qamats meteg qof sheva dalet tevir vav dagesh
changeuni: vav dagesh pe qamats merkha qof sheva dalet tevir vav dagesh
notes: The near-vertical line on the pe has a very slight northeast-southwest inclination.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the pe.

description: Change meteg under bet to merkha. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav dagesh bet qamats meteg kaf sheva tav qamats tevir he
changeuni: vav dagesh bet qamats merkha kaf sheva tav qamats tevir he
notes: The near-vertical line on the bet has a slight northeast-southwest inclination.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the bet.

description: Change meteg under aleph to merkha. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva alef qamats meteg mem sheva resh tevir vav dagesh
changeuni: vav sheva alef qamats merkha mem sheva resh tevir vav dagesh
notes: The near-vertical line on the aleph has a slight curvature, northeast to southwest. Its inclination is comparable to that of the tipeha on the same word.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha rather than a meteg under the aleph.

description: Change sheva under yod to hiriq; change munah on shin to meteg. Add note 't'.
refuni: yod sheva he vav holam shin shin-dot qubuts munah ayin patah maqaf
changeuni: yod hiriq he vav holam shin shin-dot qubuts meteg ayin patah maqaf
notes: The text of this word is of poor quality. Although the usual spelling of Joshua has a sheva under the yod, the LC has only one dot under the yod. The shuruq under the shin is unclear; the other mark under the shin is a meteg. The furtive patah for the ayin is too advanced.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a sheva under the yod and a meteg under the shin.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word. Add note 'c'.
refuni: bet dagesh hiriq final-nun maqaf
changeuni: bet dagesh hiriq final-nun
notes: A maqaf is expected at end of word.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a maqaf at end of word.

description: Place telisha gedola over tsadi.
refuni: he telisha-gedola vav holam tsadi hiriq yod alef hiriq yod
changeuni: he telisha-gedola vav holam tsadi hiriq telisha-gedola yod alef hiriq yod
notes: The circle over the aleph is a masora circle; it's too far above the aleph to be a telisha gedola.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a telisha gedola over the tsadi.

description: Move etnahta from under bet to under the kaf.
refuni: yod hiriq shin shin-dot sheva kaf dagesh qamats bet etnahta vav dagesh final-nun
changeuni: yod hiriq shin shin-dot sheva kaf dagesh qamats etnahta bet vav dagesh final-nun
notes: The etnahta clearly belongs to the kaf; space was available to place it under the bet if that was the intent.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has the etnahta under the bet.

description: Add qadma to kaf. Add note 'c'.
refuni: kaf dagesh qamats lamed maqaf
changeuni: kaf dagesh qamats qadma lamed maqaf
notes: The image shows a poetic word composed of 3 orthographic words connected by 2 maqafs. The orthographic word in question is the middle word, כָּל־.
The qadma is faint but well-formed. The aleph on the preceding orthographic word has no accent, the aleph on the following orthographic word has a zaqef qatan.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a qadma on the initial aleph and a zaqef qatan on the second aleph of this poetic word.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word. Add note 'c'.
refuni: vav sheva alef segol tav maqaf
changeuni: vav sheva alef segol tav
notes: The large black dot at the end of the word it is not shaped like a maqaf nor does it bridge to the next word.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a maqaf ending this word.

description: Change meteg on pe to merkha.
refuni: pe dagesh qamats meteg shin shin-dot sheva tet tevir vav dagesh
changeuni: pe dagesh qamats merkha shin shin-dot sheva tet tevir vav dagesh
notes: The mark under the pe has a distinct northeast to southeast inclination and is thus a merkha.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha on the pe.

description: Move merkha from vav to yod.
refuni: vav patah merkha yod dagesh holam alef mem segol resh
changeuni: vav patah merkha yod dagesh holam alef mem segol resh
notes: The mark under the yod has a slight northeast to southeast inclination and is thus a merkha.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha on the vav.
changeuni_gen: vav patah yod dagesh holam merkha alef mem segol resh

description: Remove maqaf at end of word. Add note 'c'.
refuni: alef patah het hataf-patah resh tsere yod maqaf
changeuni: alef patah het hataf-patah resh tsere yod
notes: No line is present between the yod of this word and the kaf of the next word.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a maqaf at the end of this word.

description: Add telisha qetana to yod.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh holam alef mem sheva resh vav dagesh telisha-qetana
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh holam telisha-qetana alef mem sheva resh vav dagesh telisha-qetana
notes: The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a telisha qetana on the yod.

description: Meteg on second mem may be a merkha. Add note 'm'. Remove maqaf at end of word. Add note 'c'.
refuni: mem hiriq mem dagesh patah meteg ayin hataf-patah resh tsere he maqaf
changeuni: mem hiriq mem dagesh patah meteg ayin hataf-patah resh tsere he
notes: The vertical mark under the second mem lacks the northeast-to-southwest inclination to be a merkha. No maqaf is apparent at the end of this word. The following word, which is on the next line, has no "leading" maqaf.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha under the second mem. The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has no maqaf on this word.

description: Add qadma to first lamed.
refuni: lamed hiriq mem sheva samekh hiriq lamed dagesh qamats zaqef-qatan he
changeuni: lamed hiriq qadma mem sheva samekh hiriq lamed dagesh qamats zaqef-qatan he
notes: A dark accent is visible on the first lamed. With change the word has two accents: a qadma on the first lamed (a conjunctive) and a zaqef qatan on the second lamed (level 2 disjunctive).
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a qadma on the first lamed.

description: Add geresh muqdam to initial vav. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva nun hiriq pe sheva lamed qamats alef revia vav holam tav
changeuni: vav sheva geresh-muqdam nun hiriq pe sheva lamed qamats alef revia vav holam tav
notes: The geresh muqdam is very faint at the top left of the initial vav. It appears to be doubled, possibly the outer edges of a single stroke.

description: Add mahapakh to pe, change merkha under qof to meteg. Add CGJ between meteg and patah on qof to preserve ordering.
refuni: pe dagesh qamats qof patah merkha dalet sheva tav dagesh qamats
changeuni: pe dagesh qamats mahapakh qof meteg combining-grapheme-joiner patah dalet sheva tav dagesh qamats
notes: The mark below the qamats on the pe definitely belongs to the pe, not the line below. The accent under the qof has a slight brush mark at the top but has no northeast-to-southwest inclination. A combining grapheme joiner (CGJ) has been placed between the meteg and the patah to force the meteg-first ordering.
The UXLC transcription preserves the two-dimensional mark order of the meteg relative to the patah. Other texts may not choose to maintain this ordering as a matter of editorial policy.

description: Move mahapakh from ayin to dalet.
refuni: ayin tsere mahapakh dalet vav dagesh tav space paseq
changeuni: ayin tsere dalet mahapakh vav dagesh tav space paseq

description: Remove geresh-muqdam from over ayin.
refuni: ayin holam geresh-muqdam lamed qamats lamed patah revia yod hiriq final-kaf sheva
changeuni: ayin holam lamed qamats lamed patah revia yod hiriq final-kaf sheva
notes: The preceding word, on the previous line, has a maqaf.
The final kaf has an incomplete masorah circle and rafe above it. The UXLC transcribes neither masorah circles nor rafe marks

description: Add geresh-muqdam to nun.
refuni: nun sheva dalet qamats resh patah revia yod
changeuni: nun sheva geresh-muqdam dalet qamats resh patah revia yod
notes: The geresh-muqdam above the nun is dark and well-formed. This geresh muqdam, in addition to the revia accent, produces a revia mugrash accent, an accent pair common in the Sifrei Emet.

description: Add geresh muqdam to aleph.
refuni: alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot qubuts resh tsere revia yod nun vav dagesh
changeuni: alef hataf-patah geresh-muqdam shin shin-dot qubuts resh tsere revia yod nun vav dagesh

description: Change zaqef-qatan over ayin to revia, add note 'c'.
refuni: he patah shin shin-dot dagesh sheva mem qubuts ayin qamats zaqef-qatan he
changeuni: he patah shin shin-dot dagesh sheva mem qubuts ayin qamats revia he
notes: A single dot appears above the ayin with no sign of a second dot above it. The zaqef-qatan will be replaced by a revia to represent the single dot.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a revia over the ayin; BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Remove maqaf from word, add note 'm'.
refuni: mem holam meteg qof sheva shin shin-dot tsere yod maqaf
changeuni: mem holam meteg qof sheva shin shin-dot tsere yod
notes: No maqaf between this word and the next is evident and the meteg, as a secondary accent, is missing a primary accent.
The meteg should probably be a taken as a merkha; however, it lacks the necessary northeast-to-southwest inclination.
Ben Denckla: The BHL has a merkha on the mem and no maqaf on this word. BHL Appendix A does not comment on this word.

description: Add maqaf to end of word.
refuni: shin shin-dot segol meteg bet patah ayin
changeuni: shin shin-dot segol meteg bet patah ayin maqaf
notes: The maqaf is distinct.
Ben Denckla: The BHL has a maqaf on this word. BHL Appendix A does not comment on this word.

description: Change revia over mem to zaqef-qatan.
refuni: lamed tsere alef mem holam revia resh
changeuni: lamed tsere alef mem holam zaqef-qatan resh
notes: The lower dot of the zaqef-qatan is broken, but well-positioned.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a zaqef-qatan on the mem. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Remove mahapakh from under yod. Place qadma on yod. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh holam mahapakh alef mem segol resh
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh holam alef mem segol resh
notes: Although LC text has a smudge extending partly under the yod, it is unlikely that there is an accent under the yod. A diagonal, southeast-to-northwest blurr occurs at the top tip of the yod. This could be a qadma, providing a primary accent on the word.
The Hebrew Wikisource Miqra According to the Masorah (23 Sep 2022) has a qadma on the yod.
changeuni_gen: vav patah yod dagesh holam qadma alef mem segol resh

description: Move revia from dalet to tav.
refuni: vav sheva geresh-muqdam yod qamats dalet patah revia ayin sheva tav dagesh qamats
changeuni: vav sheva geresh-muqdam yod qamats dalet patah ayin sheva tav dagesh qamats revia
notes: The revia over the tav is merged with an accent from the preceding line.
Ben Denckla: BHL agrees that this tav has a revia (and BHL implicitly agrees that no other letter in this word has revia!). BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Add maqaf to end of word.
refuni: kaf dagesh hiriq meteg yod
changeuni: kaf dagesh hiriq meteg yod maqaf
notes: Ben Denckla: BHL agrees that a maqaf is present, forming the maqaf compound כי־הביאו. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Remove maqaf from mem. Add note 'c'.
refuni: vav sheva alef hiriq final-mem maqaf
changeuni: vav sheva alef hiriq final-mem
notes: Without the maqaf, the word lacks an accent.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a maqaf at the end of the word. BHL Appendix A has no maqaf on this word for the LC.

description: Remove maqaf from yod. Change meteg to merkha. Add note 't'.
refuni: kaf dagesh hiriq meteg yod maqaf
changeuni: kaf dagesh hiriq merkha yod
notes: The text is of poor quality for this word. The near-vertical line under the kaf has a northeast-to-southeast inclination and is transcribed as a merkha.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a meteg under the kaf and a maqaf at the end of this word; BHL Appendix A has no comment on this word.

description: Move pashta from aleph to nun.
refuni: alef qamats pashta nun holam kaf hiriq yod pashta
changeuni: alef qamats pashta nun holam kaf hiriq yod pashta
notes: Ben Denckla: BHL has the pashta on the aleph; BHL Appendix A has no comment on this word.
changeuni_gen: alef qamats nun holam pashta kaf hiriq yod pashta

description: Change merkha under het to tipeha. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva alef segol het qamats merkha dalet
changeuni: vav sheva alef segol het qamats tipeha dalet
notes: The tipeha is under the dalet; however, it's probably intended to be associated with the het. The tipeha's position is almost certainly due to the top of a lamed on the following line.
Ben Denckla: BHL has the tipeha under the het; BHL Appendix A has no comment on this word.

description: Change tipeha under sin to meteg. Add note 'm'.
refuni: nun holam shin sin-dot tsere tipeha alef
changeuni: nun holam shin sin-dot tsere meteg alef
notes: The vertical line under the sin in very slightly inclined northeast-to-southwest and might be transcribed as a merkha. This inclination is similar to that of the accent under the nun in the following word. The mark will be transcribed as a meteg.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a merkha under the sin; BHL Appendix A has no comment on this word.

description: Replace zaqef-gadol on yod with revia.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh holam zaqef-gadol alef mem segol resh
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh holam revia alef mem segol resh
notes: Ben Denckla: BHL has a revia over the yod; BHL Appendix A has no comment on this word.

description: Meteg under bet might be a merkha, add note 'm'. Remove maqaf.
refuni: bet dagesh tsere meteg yod tav maqaf
changeuni: bet dagesh tsere meteg yod tav maqaf
notes: The vertical line under the bet has insufficient northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha. The former maqaf is likely a remnant of the top bar of the tav arising from a crack in the ink. Notice the missing ink in the following lamed's knee.
Ben Denckla: BHL has meteg under the bet and ends in a maqaf; BHL Appendix A does not comment on this word.
changeuni_gen: bet dagesh tsere meteg yod tav

description: Remove sof-pasuq. Add note 'c'.
refuni: ayin segol qof sheva resh meteg vav holam final-nun sof-pasuq
changeuni: ayin segol qof sheva resh meteg vav holam final-nun
notes: This verse lacks an ending sof-pasuq.
Ben Denckla: BHL ends the word with a sof-pasuq; BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this word.

description: Change merkha under the yod to meteg. Add note 'm' .
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh merkha vav holam resh segol dalet
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh meteg vav holam resh segol dalet
notes: The vertical line under the yod lacks sufficient the northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a merkha under the yod; BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this word.

description: Remove pashta from final kaf.
refuni: he patah dalet dagesh segol pashta resh segol final-kaf sheva pashta
changeuni: he patah dalet dagesh segol pashta resh segol final-kaf sheva
notes: Ben Denckla: BHL has a pashta on the final kaf; BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this word.

description: Remove maqaf.
refuni: alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot segol resh maqaf
changeuni: alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot segol resh
notes: Ben Denckla: BHL ends this word with a maqaf; BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this word.

description: Add telisha-qetana above the shin.
refuni: he hiriq shin shin-dot dagesh qamats bet sheva ayin qamats he telisha-qetana
changeuni: he hiriq shin shin-dot dagesh qamats telisha-qetana bet sheva ayin qamats he telisha-qetana
notes: The circle on the upper left of the shin is light, but attached to the shin.
Ben Denckla: BHL has a telisha-qetana above the shin; BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this word.

description: Remove dagesh from first shin, remove munah from under second shin. Add note 'c'.
refuni: vav sheva he patah shin shin-dot dagesh hiriq shin shin-dot dagesh hiriq munah yod
changeuni: vav sheva he patah shin shin-dot hiriq shin shin-dot dagesh hiriq yod
notes: The word lacks an accent. The dagesh in the second shin is very faint.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a dagesh in the first shin and a munah under the second shin.

description: Split the word by adding a maqaf between the he and the aleph.
refuni: ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot qamats he alef tsere darga lamed
changeuni: ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot qamats he alef tsere darga lamed
notes: The LC has a distinct maqaf between the he and the aleph. The maqaf has equal darkness to the that on the preceding word. The LC has a maqaf in 5 of 12 occurrences of Asahel in 2 Samuel.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) never has a maqaf in the 12 occurrences of Asahel in 2 Samuel.
changeuni_gen: ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot qamats he maqaf alef tsere darga lamed

description: Move etnahta from under bet to under kaf.
refuni: mem hiriq shin shin-dot sheva kaf dagesh qamats bet etnahta vav holam
changeuni: mem hiriq shin shin-dot sheva kaf dagesh qamats etnahta bet vav holam
notes: The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has an etnahta under the bet.

description: Remove maqaf, merging 2 words to 1.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh hiriq meteg het patah resh maqaf space alef patah mahapakh final-pe
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh hiriq meteg het patah resh alef patah mahapakh final-pe
notes: UXLC word differs from the other texts. The UXLC does not split the 6 consonants between the resh and the aleph, lacks a maqaf after the resh, and has no sheva on the resh because neither is visible in the photo.

Ben Denckla: The BHL splits the 6 consonants between the resh and the aleph and adds a maqaf after the resh. It has no sheva on the resh. BHL Appendix A contains a p.m. (prima manus) note: "version by the first hand, changed subsequently (p. xxv)". It does NOT split the 6 consonants and has no maqaf. It does have a sheva on the resh, however. Perhaps the p.m. note refers only to the suggestion that the resh originally had a sheva under it.

description: Change qadma on nun to pashta on yod.
refuni: lamed sheva ayin tsere yod nun tsere qadma yod
changeuni: lamed sheva ayin tsere yod nun tsere yod pashta
notes: BHL has a pashta on yod. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Change munah to meteg under the aleph.
refuni: alef tsere munah yod final-nun
changeuni: alef tsere meteg yod final-nun
notes: The BHL has munah under the aleph. Surprisingly, BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM agrees with this change proposal and notes that L has meteg here.

description: Remove maqaf, merging 2 words to 1. Add note 'c'.
refuni: alef patah meteg het hataf-patah resh tsere yod maqaf space kaf tsere zaqef-qatan final-nun
changeuni: alef patah meteg het hataf-patah resh tsere yod kaf tsere zaqef-qatan final-nun
notes: UXLC word differs from the other texts. The UXLC does not split the 6 consonants between the resh and the yod also lacks a maqaf after the resh because no maqaf is visible in the photo.
The Sefaria MAM has a maqaf between the resh and the yod.
Ben Denckla: The BHL splits the 6 consonants between the resh and the yod and adds a maqaf after the resh. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Move zaqef-qatan from het to dalet. Add 'c' note.
refuni: he patah het segol zaqef-qatan dalet segol resh
changeuni: he patah het segol dalet segol zaqef-qatan resh
notes: Ben Denckla: BHL has zaqef-qatan on ḥet. BHL Appendix A agrees that L has zaqef-qatan on dalet.

description: Change merkha to tevir under bet.
refuni: yod qamats bet merkha vav holam alef
changeuni: yod qamats bet tevir vav holam alef
notes: The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a tevir under the bet.

description: Change merkha under shin to tipeha.
refuni: kaf dagesh patah alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot segol merkha resh
changeuni: kaf dagesh patah alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot segol tipeha resh
notes: The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha under the shin.

description: Change tipeha under tet to meteg. Add note 'm'.
refuni: tet tipeha vav holam bet
changeuni: tet meteg vav holam bet
notes: The vertical line under the tet lacks the northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a tipeha under the tet.

description: Add zaqef-qatan above shin. Add note 'c'.
refuni: he patah shin shin-dot dagesh holam ayin tsere zaqef-qatan resh
changeuni: he patah shin shin-dot dagesh holam ayin tsere zaqef-qatan resh
notes: The shin has a dark and well-positioned zaqef-qatan over it. This accent is unexpected here.
The BHL has no zaqef-qatan on the shin. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM appears to support the zaqef-qatan over the shin.
changeuni_gen: he patah shin shin-dot dagesh holam zaqef-qatan ayin tsere zaqef-qatan resh

description: Replace tipeha under nun with merkha.
refuni: vav sheva ayin tsere yod nun segol tipeha yod final-kaf qamats
changeuni: vav sheva ayin tsere yod nun segol merkha yod final-kaf qamats
notes: The vertical line under the nun has sufficient northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha.
The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a merkha under the nun.

description: Replace merkha under first mem with tipeha.
refuni: resh qamats mem hiriq merkha yod final-mem
changeuni: resh qamats mem hiriq tipeha yod final-mem
notes: The Miqra According to the Masorah (MAM) has a tipeha under the first mem.

description: Change pashta to qadma to final kaf.
refuni: ayin patah bet sheva dalet dagesh sheva final-kaf qamats pashta
changeuni: ayin patah bet sheva dalet dagesh sheva final-kaf qamats qadma
notes: The BHL has a qadma on the final kaf. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Add qadma to kaf.
refuni: kaf dagesh qamats lamed maqaf
changeuni: kaf dagesh qamats qadma lamed maqaf
notes: The accent could also be a pashta depending on how its horizontal position is interpreted.
The BHL has a qadma on the kaf. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM positions this mark as a qadma in its transcription of the LC.

description: Change mahapakh under resh to munah.
refuni: vav sheva ayin segol shin sin-dot sheva resh hiriq mahapakh yod final-mem
changeuni: vav sheva ayin segol shin sin-dot sheva resh hiriq munah yod final-mem
notes: This change is the reverse of the change for 1 Kings 6:3.6. Here under the resh the mahapakh becomes a munah; in 1 Kings 6:3.6 a munah becomes a mahapakh.
The BHL has hiriq, munah on the resh. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Change munah under resh to mahapakh.
refuni: ayin segol shin sin-dot sheva resh hiriq munah yod final-mem
changeuni: ayin segol shin sin-dot sheva resh hiriq mahapakh yod final-mem
notes: This change is the reverse of the change for 1 Kings 6:2.10. Here under the resh the munah becomes a mahapakh; in 1 Kings 6:2.10 a mahapakh becomes a munah.
The BHL has a mahapakh, not a munah, on the resh. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Remove maqaf.
refuni: alef segol tav maqaf
changeuni: alef segol tav
notes: The BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM reads LC as having no maqaf between את and הכרובים.

description: Change pashta on aleph to qadma on tsadi.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh hiriq mem sheva tsadi qamats alef pashta
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh hiriq mem sheva tsadi qamats qadma alef
notes: Although the position of the accent on the tsadi is late, the accent must be a qadma as it has no matching final pashta.
The BHL has a qadma on the tsadi. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Replace tipeha and patah from under resh with qamats. Poor text condition, add note 't'.
refuni: alef segol pe sheva resh patah tipeha yod hiriq final-mem
changeuni: alef segol pe sheva resh qamats yod hiriq final-mem
notes: The text is in poor condition; no tipeha is visible under the resh. This leaves the word without an accent. The blob in the second consonant could be mistaken for a dagesh; however, BHL and UXLC view it as part of a pe that was disconnected from the rest of the pe.
The BHL has a qamats (not a patah) and tipeha under the resh. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM supports the replacement of the pataḥ with qamats; however, it asserts that there is a tipeḥa under the resh.

description: Add meteg under tav.
refuni: ayin qamats meteg tav dagesh qamats he sof-pasuq
changeuni: ayin qamats meteg tav dagesh qamats meteg he sof-pasuq
notes: The BHL has a meteg only on the ayin; it has no meteg on the tav. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM reads the L as in the proposed change with an unexpected second meteg.
Above, we use meteg without distinguishing it from siluq.

description: Move qadma on vav to the bet.
refuni: vav dagesh qadma bet sheva het patah tet dagesh qamats alef tav zaqef-qatan vav holam
changeuni: vav dagesh bet sheva qadma het patah tet dagesh qamats alef tav zaqef-qatan vav holam
notes: This change is analogous to that on 1 Kings 15:34.8.
The BHL has a qadma on the initial vav. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM transcribes the LC as having qadma on bet.

description: Move qadma above initial vav to following bet.
refuni: vav dagesh qadma bet sheva het patah tet dagesh qamats alef tav zaqef-qatan vav holam
changeuni: vav dagesh bet sheva qadma het patah tet dagesh qamats alef tav zaqef-qatan vav holam
notes: This change is analogous to that on 1 Kings 15:26.8.
On the initial vav, BHL has a qadma in addition to the central dot (Unicode: dagesh) that makes the vav a shuruq. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM transcribes the LC as having qadma on bet.

description: Change hataf-segol below the aleph to a hataf-patah. Remove munah below he. Add note 't'.
refuni: alef hataf-segol lamed holam he tsere munah yod
changeuni: alef hataf-patah lamed holam he tsere yod
notes: This section of text is of poor quality. No segol-like accent appears below the aleph. No munah is apparent below the he.
The BHL has a hataf-segol on the aleph and a tsere followed by munah on the he. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Remove qadma above the kaf. Add note 't'.
refuni: mem hiriq kaf dagesh holam qadma lamed
changeuni: mem hiriq kaf dagesh holam lamed
notes: This section of text is of poor quality. Two words are shown in the image; the first word is word 13, the subject of this change. The dagesh and the holam on the kaf are very faint. Comparing the lameds in both words shows no evidence of an accent on the final lamed of word 13.
The BHL has a holam and a dagesh on the kaf. It also has a pashta on the lamed. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Replace munah under lamed with meteg. Add maqaf to end of word. Add note 't'.
refuni: lamed hiriq munah yod
changeuni: lamed hiriq meteg yod maqaf
notes: This section of text is of poor quality. Note the verse has two words of lamed followed by yod; this word is the first lamed followed by yod. No munah is apparent on the lamed, the hiriq and meteg are very faint.
The BHL has a hiriq followed by munah under the lamed. It has no maqaf at the end of the word. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word; it does have an entry for word 16 in this verse, however.

description: Change munah under the bet to a mahapakh.
refuni: kaf dagesh hiriq dalet sheva bet patah munah resh
changeuni: kaf dagesh hiriq dalet sheva bet patah mahapakh resh
notes: The BHL has a patah then munah under the bet. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM transcribes the LC as having a mahapakh under the bet.

description: Remove tipeha under aleph. Add note 't'
refuni: resh sheva alef hiriq tipeha yod
changeuni: resh sheva alef hiriq yod
notes: The text quality is very poor here. The top, left side of the lamed on the following line might be taken as an accent on the aleph or yod. No tipeha under the aleph is apparent.
The BHL has a sheva under the resh and a hiriq and tipeha under the aleph. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Replace merkha under het with tipeha.
refuni: het patah merkha yod
changeuni: het patah tipeha yod
notes: The BHL has a patah and merkha under the het. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Move mahapakh under gimel to dalet.
refuni: he patah gimel dagesh qamats mahapakh dalet vav holam lamed
changeuni: he patah gimel dagesh qamats dalet mahapakh vav holam lamed
notes: The BHL has a mahapakh under the dalet. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Change merkha under nun to meteg. Add maqaf to end of word.
refuni: nun holam merkha kaf patah het
changeuni: nun holam meteg kaf patah het maqaf
notes: The vertical line under the nun has a insufficient northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha. The BHL has a meteg under the nun; thus no 'm' note has been added.
The BHL has a meteg under the nun and a maqaf at the end of the word. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.
Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM transcribes the LC as having meteg-maqaf (נֹֽכַח־) on this word.

description: Move tevir from tav to yod.
refuni: bet dagesh patah ayin hataf-patah lamed hiriq yod dagesh qamats tav tevir vav holam
changeuni: bet dagesh patah ayin hataf-patah lamed hiriq yod dagesh qamats tevir tav vav holam
notes: The sheva mark under the bet must belong to the patah under the ayin in the usual form of hataf-patah.
The BHL has a tevir on the tav. BHL Appendix A has the tevir on the yod. Both BHL and BHL Appendix A have a patah under the bet and a hataf-patah under the ayin.

description: Change accent over kaf from zaqef-qatan to revia.
refuni: mem patah lamed sheva alef qamats kaf hiriq zaqef-qatan yod final-mem
changeuni: mem patah lamed sheva alef qamats kaf hiriq revia yod final-mem
notes: The BHL has a revia on the kaf. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word, but has an entry for word 5.

description: Change merkha under nun to tipeha.
refuni: mem hiriq pe dagesh sheva nun tsere merkha yod
changeuni: mem hiriq pe dagesh sheva nun tsere tipeha yod
notes: The BHL has a tipeha under the nun. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Change tipeha under aleph to meteg. Add note 'm'.
refuni: het hataf-patah zayin qamats alef tsere tipeha lamed
changeuni: het hataf-patah zayin qamats alef tsere meteg lamed
notes: The vertical line under the aleph lacks sufficient northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha.
The BHL has a merkha under the aleph, not a tipeha. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this word.

description: Replace sheva on shin with patah. Add 't' note. Replace merkha on lamed with meteg. Add 'm' note.
refuni: vav dagesh meteg shin shin-dot sheva lamed patah merkha het
changeuni: vav dagesh meteg shin shin-dot patah lamed patah meteg het
notes: The vowel under the shin is hard to determine. A patah is a better fit than a sheva.

A blob emerges from the top of the lamed of a word on the following line; word 22 of this verse. The blob might be considered in determining the markings on the shin of this word, word 19. There is a similar blob emerging from the lamed of the 5-th word of this verse. Consequently, the blob is considered to be an odd part of the lamed of word 22 and not part of the markings on the shin of word 19.
The BHL has a hataf patah under the shin and a merkha under the lamed. BHL Appendix A has a patah under the shin.

description: Replace merkha under pe with tipeha.
refuni: vav sheva kaf patah pe dagesh merkha vav holam tav
changeuni: vav sheva kaf patah pe dagesh merkha vav holam tav
notes: The BHL has merkha under the pe. BHL Appendix A has a tipeha under the pe.
changeuni_gen: vav sheva kaf patah pe dagesh tipeha vav holam tav

description: Remove munah from under shin.
refuni: alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot segol munah resh
changeuni: alef hataf-patah shin shin-dot segol resh
notes: This word does not have a maqaf in the LC and is therefore lacking an accent.
The BHL has no accent under the shin and a final maqaf. BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word; it has an entry for word 10.

description: Change hataf-patah under ayin to patah. Change merkha under shin to meteg. Add note 'm'.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh patah meteg ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot segol merkha he
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh patah meteg ayin patah shin sin-dot segol meteg he
notes: The vertical line under the shin lacks sufficient northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha.
The BHL has a patah then meteg under yod, a hataf-patah under the ayin, and a segol then merkha under the shin (as shown in the Reference text above); BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace revia on samekh with telisha-gedola.
refuni: samekh qamats revia resh
changeuni: samekh qamats telisha-gedola resh
notes: Although the "tail" of the telisha isn't visible, the circle in the image is prepositive. Hence it is transcribed as a telisha-gedola.
The BHL has a telisha-gedola above the samekh; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace munah under samekh with meteg. Add note 't'.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva samekh tsere munah pe segol resh
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva samekh tsere meteg pe segol resh
notes: The vertical line under the samekh has a slight northwest-to-southeast inclination and might be transcribed as a tipeha. A tipeha is unexpected here, however.

An erasure appears to have occurred at the lower, left corner of the line under the samekh. This erasure might have been of the horizontal line of a munah. The vertical line of a munah has a slight northwest-to-southeast inclination and if the horizontal line of a munah is erased, the result would have the shape shown in the image; a slightly inclined meteg. The immediately preceding word, on the preceding line, has a munah. The accent under the samekh is transcribed here as a meteg reflecting this possibility and marked with a 't' note.
The BHL has meteg under the samekh; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

Ben Denckla: Breuer-DM reveals that there is a tradition, attested in the Cairo Codex of the Prophets and other sources, of having munaḥ-space on this word (בְּסֵ֣פֶר) instead of meteg-maqaf (בְּסֵֽפֶר־).

description: Remove geresh from kaf.
refuni: vav patah yod dagesh tsere qadma lamed sheva kaf geresh vav dagesh
changeuni: vav patah yod dagesh tsere qadma lamed sheva kaf vav dagesh
notes: The BHL has a geresh above the kaf; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace revia on mem with zaqef-qatan.
refuni: shin shin-dot qamats mem patah revia ayin sheva tav dagesh qamats
changeuni: shin shin-dot qamats mem patah zaqef-qatan ayin sheva tav dagesh qamats
notes: The BHL has a zaqef-qatan above the mem; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace zaqef-qatan above shin with revia. Add note 't'.
refuni: mem sheva nun patah shin shin-dot dagesh segol zaqef-qatan he
changeuni: mem sheva nun patah shin shin-dot dagesh segol revia he
notes: A faint blurr above the left-most dot above the shin may have made the accent a zaqef-qatan. The accent will be transcribed as a revia.
The BHL has a revia above the shin; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace revia above kaf with telisha-gedola.
refuni: kaf dagesh hiriq revia yod
changeuni: kaf dagesh hiriq telisha-gedola yod
notes: The BHL has a telisha-gedola above the kaf; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Remove qadma above nun, add pashta to he.
refuni: shin shin-dot qamats nun qamats qadma he
changeuni: shin shin-dot qamats nun qamats he pashta
notes: The BHL has a pashta above the he; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace tipeha under the lamed with tevir.
refuni: ayin qamats lamed segol tipeha yod he qamats
changeuni: ayin qamats lamed segol tevir yod he qamats
notes: The BHL has a segol then tevir on the lamed; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Replace merkha under the yod with tipeha.
refuni: dalet dagesh qamats yod tsere merkha qof
changeuni: dalet dagesh qamats yod tsere tipeha qof
notes: The BHL has a tipeha on the yod; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Move munah under resh to under the bet.
refuni: shin shin-dot hiriq bet dagesh sheva resh munah vav dagesh
changeuni: shin shin-dot hiriq bet dagesh sheva munah resh vav dagesh
notes: The BHL has a munah under the resh; BHL Appendix A has no entries for this word.

description: Change merkha on bet to tipeha.
refuni: tet vav holam bet qamats merkha he
changeuni: tet vav holam bet qamats tipeha he

description: Change revia on tsadi to zaqef qatan.
refuni: vav dagesh mem patah tsadi dagesh hiriq revia lamed
changeuni: vav dagesh mem patah tsadi dagesh hiriq zaqef-qatan lamed
notes: BHL has a zaqef qatan over the tsadi. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move mahapakh from nun to dalet.
refuni: vav sheva nun qamats mahapakh dalet vav holam shin shin-dot
changeuni: vav sheva nun qamats dalet mahapakh vav holam shin shin-dot
notes: The mahapakh is well-removed from the nun.
BHL has the mahapakh on the dalet. BHL Appendix A has no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: resh patah bet maqaf
changeuni: resh patah bet
notes: The gap between the bet and the shin is a good word space as recognized in BHL Appendix A. This leaves this word without an accent, however.
BHL has a maqaf after the bet. BHL Appendix A has a space rather than a maqaf after the bet.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: resh patah bet maqaf
changeuni: resh patah bet
notes: The maqaf of the preceding word is clear; this word has no maqaf.
BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no maqaf after the bet.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: resh patah bet maqaf
changeuni: resh patah bet
notes: BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no maqaf after the bet.

description: Move tipeha from final kaf to tav.
refuni: vav segol he hataf-segol mem hiriq yod tav sheva final-kaf qamats tipeha
changeuni: vav segol he hataf-segol mem hiriq yod tav sheva tipeha final-kaf qamats
notes: BHL has the tipeha on the final kaf. BHL Appendix A has the tipeha on the tav.

description: Move pashta from first yod to second yod.
refuni: he qamats yod hiriq pashta yod tav hiriq yod pashta
changeuni: he qamats yod hiriq yod pashta tav hiriq yod pashta
notes: BHL has a pashta over the first yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: ayin patah lamed maqaf
changeuni: ayin patah lamed
notes: BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no maqaf at the end of this word.

description: Change merkha under qof to tipeha.
refuni: bet dagesh patah mem dagesh qamats qof merkha vav holam final-mem
changeuni: bet dagesh patah mem dagesh qamats qof tipeha vav holam final-mem
notes: BHL has a tipeha on the qof. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Replace zaqef qatan with revia. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva meteg nun qamats alef holam zaqef-qatan final-pe
changeuni: vav sheva meteg nun qamats alef holam revia final-pe
notes: The text is in bad condition for this word. The markings above the alef are hard to recognize. The image more nearly supports a revia rather than a zaqef qatan.
BHL has a zaqef-qatan on the alef. BHL Appendix A has a revia on the alef and a '?' note.

description: Change revia on the second samekh to zaqef qatan.
refuni: samekh vav dagesh samekh qamats revia yod vav
changeuni: samekh vav dagesh samekh qamats revia yod vav
notes: The accent on the second samekh has two dots. Compare with the zaqef qatan of the following line shown below the first samekh.
BHL has zaqef qatan on the samekh. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.
changeuni_gen: samekh vav dagesh samekh qamats zaqef-qatan yod vav

description: Change tipeha under the lamed to a merkha.
refuni: mem hiriq bet dagesh sheva lamed hiriq tipeha yod
changeuni: mem hiriq bet dagesh sheva lamed hiriq merkha yod
notes: The line under the lamed has the distinct northeast-to-southwest inclination of a merkha.
BHL has merkha under the lamed. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change tipeha under lamed to meteg. Add transcription note 'm'.
refuni: mem hiriq bet dagesh sheva lamed hiriq tipeha yod
changeuni: mem hiriq bet dagesh sheva lamed hiriq meteg yod
notes: The accent under the lamed is a vertical line. It is probably a merkha but will be replaced by a meteg and transcription note 'm'.
BHL has a merkha under the lamed. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva ayin tsere merkha tav maqaf
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva ayin tsere merkha tav
notes: BHL has no maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: he qamats ayin qamats mahapakh final-mem maqaf
changeuni: he qamats ayin qamats mahapakh final-mem
notes: The text in this region has light smudges in many places. The ink of the text is dark and shows no maqaf at the end of the word.
BHL has no maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move munah from bet to yod.
refuni: yod qamats bet munah vav holam alef
changeuni: yod qamats munah bet munah vav holam alef
notes: The accents are crowded under the yod. Plenty of space is available under the bet if that was the scribe's intention. This change is similar to that on Jer 17:8.14 below.
BHL has a qamats and munah under the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.
changeuni_gen: yod qamats munah bet vav holam alef

description: Move munah from bet to yod.
refuni: yod qamats bet holam munah alef
changeuni: yod qamats munah bet holam alef
notes: This change is similar to that on Jer 17:6.7 above.
BHL has a qamats and munah under the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Replace munah under the bet with merkha.
refuni: vav sheva nun patah het sheva shin shin-dot sheva bet qamats munah he
changeuni: vav sheva nun patah het sheva shin shin-dot sheva bet qamats merkha he
notes: The markings under the bet are influenced by the top of a lamed on the following line. The qamats under the bet is advanced and the merkha is retarded to avoid the top of the lamed. The line under the bet has sufficient northeast-to-southwest inclination to be a merkha. The merkha line is unusually thin.
BHL has a qamats and merkha under the bet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.
Aside: The lower dot of the sheva under the shin has been overwritten by a masorah circle on the following line. BHL has a sheva under the shin.

description: Change zaqef qatan on kaf to revia. Add note 't.
refuni: shin shin-dot hiriq kaf dagesh zaqef-qatan vav holam resh
changeuni: shin shin-dot hiriq kaf dagesh revia vav holam resh
notes: The marking on the kaf lacks a sufficiently dark upper dot to be transcribed as a zaqef qatan. It will be transcribed as a revia with a 't' note.
BHL has a zaqef qatan on the kaf. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change qadma on resh to pashta on yod. Add note 't'.
refuni: dalet dagesh hiriq bet sheva resh tsere qadma yod
changeuni: dalet dagesh hiriq bet sheva resh tsere yod pashta
notes: The accent starts at the top of the yod, rather than the sloping later part of the yod. Distinguishing between a qadma or pashta is difficult on such a thin letter. Pashtas occur more frequently on the final letter of a word than qadmas. Ben Denckla offers a cantillation rule: "When a qadma/pashta appears on a letter that closes a syllable and ends a word ... it should be interpreted as a pashta". Given the uncertainty and need to transcribe the accent, the accent is transcribed here as a pashta with a 't' added.

BHL has a qadma/pashta on the yod, not the resh. The difference between these two accents is hard to determine on such a thin consonant, even on a printed page. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change gershayim above the bet to a zaqef gadol.
refuni: shin shin-dot qamats bet patah gershayim resh sheva tav dagesh hiriq yod
changeuni: shin shin-dot qamats bet patah zaqef-gadol resh sheva tav dagesh hiriq yod
notes: The BHL has a zaqef gadol over the bet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change merkha under kaf to tevir. Add 'c' note.
refuni: nun sheva bet hiriq meteg yod alef tsere yod kaf segol merkha final-mem
changeuni: nun sheva bet hiriq meteg yod alef tsere yod kaf segol tevir final-mem
notes: The dot on the tevir is well-sized, well-positioned, and dark; however, it is slightly brownish.
The BHL has a tevir under the kaf. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

Because the preceding word, לָכֶ֛ם, has a tevir accent, this change produces two consecutive words with tevir accents. Although these markings occur in BHL without comment, they're unexpected according to current cantillation rules. Hence, a 'c' note is added.

description: Move zaqef qatan from over the nun to over the yod.
refuni: vav sheva meteg nun hiriq bet sheva nun tsere zaqef-qatan yod tav
changeuni: vav sheva meteg nun hiriq bet sheva nun tsere yod zaqef-qatan tav
notes: The lower dot of the zaqef qatan contacts the top of the yod.

The BHL and UXLC have different versification of Jeremiah chapters 30 and 31. In the BHL chapter 30 has 25 verses; the UXLC has 24 verses. BHL verse 30:25 is UXLC verse 31:1; hence this UXLC verse, 31.4, is BHL verse 31.3. The BHL has a zaqef qatan on the nun. BHL Appendix A has the zaqef qatan on the yod.

description: Change tipeha under alef to munah.
refuni: mem tsere alef segol tipeha resh segol final-tsadi
changeuni: mem tsere alef segol munah resh segol final-tsadi
notes: This is BHL Jeremiah 31:31. The BHL has a munah under the alef. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: vav sheva ayin holam merkha shin sin-dot segol he maqaf
changeuni: vav sheva ayin holam merkha shin sin-dot segol he
notes: The text is in good condition around this word. The space between this word and the next is slightly darker than the spaces before the current word and following the subsequent word. This may be an erased maqaf.
The BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change yetiv on lamed to mahapakh.
refuni: lamed holam yetiv alef
changeuni: lamed holam mahapakh alef
notes: A yetiv is prepositive, that is, a yetiv appears on the leading edge of a consonant. This accent is not prepositive and is therefore a mahapakh.
The BHL has a mahapakh on the lamed. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Add revia above the yod.
refuni: vav dagesh bet sheva yod patah dalet
changeuni: vav dagesh bet sheva yod patah revia dalet
notes: A previous change in Changes file 2021.10.19 - Changes, change 2021.08.07 - 7, had an incorrect image for this word. The image was of word 10. The error has been corrected in the Changes file 2021.10.19 - Changes on 15 Jan 2023.
This word, 13, has a revia above the yod. The transcription note 'c' from the previous change, which was based on an incorrect image, will be removed.
The BHL has a revia above the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change merkha to tipeha under tet.
refuni: lamed sheva mem hiriq qof dagesh qamats tet holam merkha final-nun
changeuni: lamed sheva mem hiriq qof dagesh qamats tet holam tipeha final-nun
notes: The BHL has a tipeha under the tet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change merkha under het to tipeha.
refuni: bet dagesh patah het segol merkha resh segol bet
changeuni: bet dagesh patah het segol tipeha resh segol bet
notes: The next word Jer 44:13.11 changes a tipeha to a merkha. That is, the accents on Jer 44:13.10 and Jer 44:13.11 are interchanged.
The BHL has merkha under the het. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change tipeha under ayin to merkha.
refuni: bet dagesh qamats resh qamats ayin qamats tipeha bet
changeuni: bet dagesh qamats resh qamats ayin qamats merkha bet
notes: The vertical line under the ayin has a very slight northeast-to-southwest inclination.
The previous word Jer 44:13.10 was changed from a merkha to tipeha. That is, the accents on Jer 44:13.10 and Jer 44:13.11 are interchanged.
The BHL has tipeha under the ayin. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move telisha qetana on kaf to yod.
refuni: kaf dagesh hiriq telisha-qetana yod
changeuni: kaf dagesh hiriq yod telisha-qetana
notes: The BHL has a telisha-qetana on the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva alef patah het hataf-patah resh tsere yod maqaf
changeuni: vav sheva alef patah het hataf-patah resh tsere yod
notes: No maqaf is visible after the yod in this word. Without a maqaf, this word lacks an accent.
The BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no maqaf at the end of this word.

description: Change zaqef gadol over first yod to zaqef qatan.
refuni: vav sheva tav hiriq meteg he sheva yod segol zaqef-gadol yod nun qamats he
changeuni: vav sheva tav hiriq meteg he sheva yod segol zaqef-qatan yod nun qamats he
notes: The vertical bar on the trailing edge of the zaqef qatan dots might have been part of an erased zaqef gadol.
The BHL has a zaqef qatan over the first yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Add pashta over final vav.
refuni: alef hataf-patah resh hiriq yod tsadi segol qadma nun dagesh vav dagesh
changeuni: alef hataf-patah resh hiriq yod tsadi segol qadma nun dagesh vav dagesh pashta
notes: This word is in a dark spot on the page. With care, a trailing pashta can be seen on the vav.
The BHL has an accent-helper pashta over the tsadi and a pashta on the vav. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change revia on pe to zaqef qatan.
refuni: he patah kaf dagesh patah pe dagesh revia vav holam tav
changeuni: he patah kaf dagesh patah pe dagesh zaqef-qatan vav holam tav
notes: The lower dot of the zaqef touches the top of the pe.
The BHL has revia over the pe. BHL Appendix A has a zaqef qatan over the pe.

description: Add telisha qetana over shin.
refuni: bet dagesh patah ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot vav holam tav hiriq yod telisha-qetana
changeuni: bet dagesh patah ayin hataf-patah shin sin-dot telisha-qetana vav holam tav hiriq yod telisha-qetana
notes: This mark serves as a "stress helper" for the postpositive telisha qetana.
The BHL has a telisha qetana over the shin. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Add maqaf to end of word.
refuni: ayin patah tipeha lamed
changeuni: ayin patah tipeha lamed maqaf
notes: The BHL has no maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has a maqaf at the end of the word.

description: Change zaqef qatan on he to revia.
refuni: alef hataf-patah lamed tsere he segol zaqef-qatan final-mem
changeuni: alef hataf-patah lamed tsere he segol revia final-mem
notes: The text is of poor quality. A dot above the revia (making it a zaqef qatan) may have been erased.
The BHL has a revia over the he. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove qadma from above ayin.
refuni: vav sheva ayin patah qadma lamed maqaf
changeuni: vav sheva ayin patah lamed maqaf
notes: The images shows two maqaf-connected words: ועל דמך. This change applies to the first word of these words, ועל. The following word, דמך, has a zaqef qatan on the mem.

Ben Denckla: The Unicode qadma removed here would have functioned as an accent called a "metigah". A metigah exists only as part of a two-accent-on-a-poetic-word combination called a metigah-zaqef. The zaqef in a metigah-zaqef is always a zaqef qatan. This change removes a metigah-zaqef from these two words; the poetic word formed from these two maqaf-connected words continues to have an accent, a zaqef qatan.

The BHL has no qadma on the ayin. It does have a zaqef qatan on the mem of the following word. That is, the BHL does not have a metigah-zaqef on this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move zaqef qatan above bet to shin.
refuni: mem hiriq bet dagesh qamats zaqef-qatan shin shin-dot qamats final-nun
changeuni: mem hiriq bet dagesh qamats shin shin-dot qamats zaqef-qatan final-nun
notes: The BHL has a zaqef qatan above the shin, not the bet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change munah below bet to meteg.
refuni: bet dagesh qamats munah ayin patah mem dagesh hiriq zaqef-qatan yod final-mem
changeuni: bet dagesh qamats meteg ayin patah mem dagesh hiriq zaqef-qatan yod final-mem
notes: The current munah is a meteg with an errant dot; it does not have munah proportions.
The BHL has a meteg under the bet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change merkha under yod to tipeha.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva yod merkha vav holam final-mem
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva yod tipeha vav holam final-mem
notes: The BHL has a tipeha under the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Add qadma over the he.
refuni: vav sheva he hiriq tav sheva qof patah dalet dagesh hiriq shin shin-dot sheva tav dagesh hiriq zaqef-qatan yod
changeuni: vav sheva he hiriq tav sheva qof patah dalet dagesh hiriq shin shin-dot sheva tav dagesh hiriq zaqef-qatan yod
notes: Ben Denckla: The Unicode qadma added here functions as an accent called a "metigah". A metigah exists only as part of a two-accent-on-a-poetic-word combination called a metigah-zaqef. The zaqef in a metigah-zaqef is always a zaqef qatan.
The BHL has qadma over the he. It also has a zaqef qatan over the second tav; that is, BHL has a metigah-zaqef on this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.
changeuni_gen: vav sheva he hiriq qadma tav sheva qof patah dalet dagesh hiriq shin shin-dot sheva tav dagesh hiriq zaqef-qatan yod

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: resh holam darga het patah bet maqaf
changeuni: resh holam darga het patah bet
notes: The BHL has no maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Add telisha qetana to lamed. Remove note 'c'.
refuni: vav sheva he patah gimel dagesh sheva bet vav dagesh lamed
changeuni: vav sheva he patah gimel dagesh sheva bet vav dagesh lamed telisha-qetana
notes: The telisha qetana is very high on the lamed.
The BHL has telisha qetana on the lamed. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove qadma from nun, add pashta to yod. Add note 't'.
refuni: pe dagesh sheva nun tsere qadma yod
changeuni: pe dagesh sheva nun tsere yod pashta
notes: The pashta/qadma accent is clearly on the yod. Pashtas occur more frequently on the final letter of a word than qadmas. Ben Denckla offers a cantillation rule: "When a qadma/pashta appears on a letter that closes a syllable and ends a word ... it should be interpreted as a pashta". Given the uncertainty and need to transcribe the accent, the accent is transcribed here as a pashta with a 't' added.
The BHL has a pashta/qadma over the nun; it's hard to tell the difference even in this modern, printed document. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: nun sheva alef qubuts final-mem maqaf
changeuni: nun sheva alef qubuts final-mem
notes: BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this book.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: lamed hiriq bet sheva nun tsere meteg yod maqaf
changeuni: lamed hiriq bet sheva nun tsere meteg yod
notes: BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this book.

description: Move meteg from yod to he.
refuni: he qamats yod meteg vav dagesh sof-pasuq
changeuni: he qamats meteg yod vav dagesh sof-pasuq
notes: BHL has a meteg under the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this book.

description: Add merkha to kaf.
refuni: lamed sheva mem patah lamed sheva kaf tsere tipeha yod
changeuni: lamed sheva mem patah lamed sheva kaf tsere merkha yod
notes: BHL has a tipeha under the kaf. BHL Appendix A has a merkha under the kaf.

description: Remove qadma on the resh, add pashta to yod.
refuni: dalet sheva bet qamats resh patah qadma yod
changeuni: dalet sheva bet qamats resh patah yod pashta
notes: The accent on the yod in the image is clearly postpositive and, therefore, a pashta.
BHL has a pashta/qadma on the yod. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: lamed sheva bet patah merkha tav maqaf
changeuni: lamed sheva bet patah merkha tav
notes: BHL has no maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move qadma over nun to he.
refuni: he patah nun dagesh hiriq qadma shin shin-dot sheva bet dagesh qamats zaqef-qatan ayin
changeuni: he patah qadma nun dagesh hiriq shin shin-dot sheva bet dagesh qamats zaqef-qatan ayin
notes: The qadma being moved is part of a metigah-zaqef accent pair.
BHL has a qadma over the he. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move tipeha under tav to under resh. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav patah alef hataf-patah mem patah resh sheva tav dagesh segol tipeha final-mem
changeuni: vav patah alef hataf-patah mem patah resh sheva tipeha tav dagesh segol final-mem
notes: Normally, accents are written after a vowel. The tipeha in the image could be considered under either the resh or the tav. Space is available after the segol to write the tipeha had the scribe intended to do so.
BHL has the tipeha under the tav positioned after the segol. BHL Appendix A has the tipeha under the tav but positioned before the segol.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: ayin patah dalet maqaf
changeuni: ayin patah dalet
notes: The image shows 3 words: עד בלי די, words 27, 28, 29 of Mal 3:10. This change is on word 27, עד. In conjunction with the subsequent change to Mal 3:10.28, this change produces three space-separated words, rather than three maqaf-separated words.
BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word; that is, words 27, 28, 29 are separated by maqafs. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf from end of word.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva lamed hiriq yod maqaf
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva lamed hiriq yod
notes: The image shows 3 words: עד בלי די, words 27, 28, 29 of Mal 3:10. This change is on word 28, בלי. In conjunction with the previous change to Mal 3:10.27, this change produces three space-separated words, rather than three maqaf-separated words.
BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word; that is, words 27, 28, 29 are separated by maqafs. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Replace mahapakh on kaf with yetiv.
refuni: kaf dagesh holam mahapakh lamed
changeuni: kaf dagesh holam yetiv lamed
notes: A yetiv is prepositive, a mahapakh is centered on the consonant.
BHL has a yetiv on the kaf. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: vav sheva ayin patah munah dalet maqaf
changeuni: vav sheva ayin patah munah dalet
notes: BHL has no maqaf at end of this word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this word.

description: Remove pashta from final mem. Add note 't'.
refuni: yod qamats mem hiriq munah yod final-mem pashta
changeuni: yod qamats mem hiriq munah yod final-mem
notes: This change does not refer to the dark, clearly-defined tipeha above the right edge of the final mem; the tipeha belongs to the word on the previous line, Deut 5:12.7. An ill-defined diagonal stub occurs on the upper, left top of the final mem. It has a possible other remnant to the left of the sheva on the previous line. It could be a remnant of an erased pashta.
UXLC verse Deut 5:13 corresponds to BHL verse Deut 5:12. BHL has a pashta on the final mem. BHL Appendix A has no pashta on the final mem.

description: Change dehi on tet to tipeha (= tarḥa).
refuni: tet dehi vav holam bet
changeuni: tet tipeha vav holam bet
notes: The mark in the LC is not sufficiently prepositive (advanced relative to the consonant) to be transcribed as a dehi.
BHL has a tipeha (=tarḥa) on the tet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move revia back from second vav to nun.
refuni: vav patah geresh-muqdam yod dagesh hiriq zayin sheva nun vav dagesh revia
changeuni: vav patah geresh-muqdam yod dagesh hiriq zayin sheva nun revia vav dagesh
notes: BHL has the revia over the nun. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move mahapakh from dalet to vav. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav sheva nun qamats mahapakh dalet vav holam shin shin-dot
changeuni: vav sheva nun qamats dalet vav holam mahapakh shin shin-dot
notes: The mahapakh most likely belongs to the dalet rather than the vav. The lack of space under the dalet likely caused the shift from the dalet to vav. The mahapakh will be transcribed under the vav to represent the text image. Although the changed text places the mahapakh under the vav, fonts may place the mahapakh slightly to the left of the vav. This is the case with the Taamey D Web font here.
BHL has the mahapakh on the dalet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove iluy from bet.
refuni: bet dagesh holam meteg iluy alef maqaf
changeuni: bet dagesh holam meteg alef maqaf
notes: An iluy has two sides and is more rectangular than the image's half circle above the bet. It is almost certainly a broken masorah circle referring to the masoretic note in the left text margin. The note, according to Ben Denckla, says “4× haser in the Neviʾim.” .
BHL has a no iluy on the bet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change mahapakh on kaf to yetiv.
refuni: kaf dagesh holam mahapakh lamed
changeuni: kaf dagesh holam yetiv lamed
notes: The mahapakh/yetiv mark is displaced to the right of the consonant and therefore a yetiv.
BHL has a distinct yetiv under the kaf. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move darga from under gimel to under dalet.
refuni: lamed sheva mem hiriq gimel dagesh qamats darga dalet vav holam lamed
changeuni: lamed sheva mem hiriq gimel dagesh qamats dalet darga vav holam lamed
notes: BHL has the darga under the dalet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Change dehi on qof to tipeha. Add note 'd'.
refuni: qof dehi vav holam lamed hiriq yod
changeuni: qof tipeha vav holam lamed hiriq yod
notes: The curved mark under the qof is not very prepositive as required for a dehi.
BHL has a clearly prepositive dehi under the qof. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move meteg from below the mem to below the tsadi. Remove CGJ. Add note 't'
refuni: ayin hataf-patah tsadi qamats mem meteg combining-grapheme-joiner qamats yod sof-pasuq
changeuni: ayin hataf-patah tsadi qamats meteg mem qamats yod sof-pasuq
notes: The meteg is centered between the tsadi and the mem.
BHL has the meteg under the mem. BHL Appendix A has the meteg under the tsadi. The meteg follows the qamats.
The CGJ is no longer needed for positioning the meteg and has been removed.

description: Replace merkha below the het with a meteg. Add transcription note 'm'.
refuni: alef qamats resh sheva het merkha vav holam tav
changeuni: alef qamats resh sheva het meteg vav holam tav
notes: The line under the het lacks the northeast-to-southwest inclination required for transcription as a merkha. The accent was probably intended to be a merkha. The spacing between this word and the next is very close.
BHL has a merkha under the het. The BHL has a meteg under the mem of the next word. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move the meteg from under the resh to under the shin. Add note 't'.
refuni: bet dagesh sheva mem tsere yod shin shin-dot qamats resh meteg combining-grapheme-joiner hiriq yod final-mem sof-pasuq
changeuni: bet dagesh sheva mem tsere yod shin shin-dot qamats meteg resh hiriq yod final-mem sof-pasuq
notes: The meteg is centered between the shin and the resh.
BHL has the meteg under the resh. BHL Appendix A has the meteg under the shin. The meteg follows the qamats.

description: Remove dagesh in nun; remove sof pasuq. Add note 't'.
refuni: mem hiriq mem dagesh segol meteg nun dagesh hiriq yod sof-pasuq
changeuni: mem hiriq mem dagesh segol meteg nun hiriq yod
notes: The nun has no dagesh. This verse ends without a sof pasuq.
BHL has a dagesh in the nun and a sof pasuq at the end of the word. BHL Appendix A has no comment on this verse.

description: Move meteg from third bet to second bet.
refuni: bet dagesh hiriq lamed sheva bet qamats bet meteg vav holam sof-pasuq
changeuni: bet dagesh hiriq lamed sheva bet qamats meteg bet vav holam sof-pasuq
notes: BHL has the meteg under the third bet. BHL Appendix A has the meteg under the second bet.

description: Remove sheva under the bet; move merkha under the ayin to under the bet.
refuni: yod hiriq shin sin-dot sheva bet dagesh sheva ayin merkha vav dagesh
changeuni: yod hiriq shin sin-dot sheva bet dagesh merkha ayin vav dagesh
notes: The near-vertical line under the bet has a very slight northeast-to-southwest inclination and will be transcribed as a merkha.
BHL has a sheva under the bet and a merkha under the ayin. BHL Appendix A has only a merkha under the bet; no sheva under the bet. BHL Appendix A has no marking under the ayin.

description: Move meteg from he to third lamed.
refuni: lamed sheva ayin vav holam lamed sheva lamed tsere yod he meteg combining-grapheme-joiner segol final-mem sof-pasuq
changeuni: lamed sheva ayin vav holam lamed sheva lamed tsere meteg yod he segol final-mem sof-pasuq
notes: The Meteg is under the third Lamed.
BHL has a meteg under the he; the meteg follows a segol. BHL Appendix A has the meteg under the third lamed; the meteg follows the tsere.

description: Change accent under ayin to meteg. Add note 'm'
refuni: samekh patah meteg lamed sheva ayin hiriq merkha yod
changeuni: samekh patah meteg lamed sheva ayin hiriq meteg yod
notes: The accents on the samekh and ayin are nearly identical and lack the northeast-to-southwest inclination to be transcribed as a merkha. Many texts have a merkha as the accent on the ayin.
BHL has a meteg under the samekh and merkha under the ayin. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this verse.

description: Change dehi under the tsadi to tipeha. Add note 'd'.
refuni: tsadi dehi vav dagesh resh hiriq yod
changeuni: tsadi tipeha vav dagesh resh hiriq yod
notes: The mark under the tsadi is not prepositive; thus it will be transcribed as a tipeha.
BHL has a dehi under the tsadi. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this verse.

description: Add maqaf at end of word.
refuni: yod hiriq meteg he sheva yod merkha vav dagesh
changeuni: yod hiriq meteg he sheva yod merkha vav dagesh maqaf
notes: Vav and yod tops in this region of text slope downward; here the vav has a horizontal component.
BHL has a maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no entry for this verse.
The exclamation mark ('!') splits the word at a previous 't' transcription note; it does not appear in the new word.

description: Remove meteg from under dalet. Add note 'c'.
refuni: alef hataf-patah he vav holam dalet segol meteg nun dagesh vav dagesh sof-pasuq
changeuni: alef hataf-patah he vav holam dalet segol nun dagesh vav dagesh sof-pasuq
notes: This final word has no meteg acting as a siluq.
BHL has a meteg under the dalet. BHL Appendix A has no meteg under the dalet or any other consonant.

description: Remove maqaf at end of word.
refuni: alef segol ole lamed maqaf
changeuni: alef segol ole lamed
notes: BHL has no maqaf at the end of this word. BHL Appendix A has no comment on this verse.

description: Remove meteg under zayin.
refuni: mem qamats meteg ayin vav dagesh zayin dagesh hiriq meteg yod sof-pasuq
changeuni: mem qamats meteg ayin vav dagesh zayin dagesh hiriq yod sof-pasuq
notes: BHL has a meteg under both the mem and the zayin. BHL Appendix A has a meteg under the mem and no meteg under the zayin.

description: Remove meteg under the bet. Add note 't'.
refuni: vav qamats bet meteg vav dagesh zayin sof-pasuq
changeuni: vav qamats bet vav dagesh zayin sof-pasuq
notes: The ascender of the lamed on the following line may be obscuring a meteg under the bet. As the last word in a verse this word should have a meteg acting as a siluq. As such, a meteg should probably be under the bet.
BHL has a meteg under the bet. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move the munah from the lamed to the bet.
refuni: bet dagesh qamats lamed munah vav dagesh
changeuni: bet dagesh qamats munah lamed vav dagesh
notes: Because of the ascender of the lamed on the following line, no space was available to write the munah under the lamed. It was probably intended to be under the lamed.
BHL has a munah under the lamed. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Move ole from tav to tsadi. Add note 't'.
refuni: tav dagesh hiriq ole tsadi dagesh sheva resh tsere merkha nun hiriq yod
changeuni: tav dagesh hiriq tsadi dagesh sheva ole resh tsere merkha nun hiriq yod
notes: The ole is very faded, but definitely over the tsadi.
BHL has the ole over the tsadi, not the tav. BHL Appendix A makes no comment on this verse.

description: Remove meteg from under the lamed. Add transcription note 'c'.
refuni: lamed meteg vav holam sof-pasuq
changeuni: lamed vav holam sof-pasuq
notes: The last word in the verse should have a meteg acting as a siluq.
BHL has a meteg under the lamed. BHL Appendix A has no meteg under the lamed.

description: Move ole from pe to lamed.
refuni: vav patah meteg yod sheva pe patah ole lamed dagesh sheva tet tsere merkha final-mem
changeuni: vav patah meteg yod sheva pe patah lamed dagesh sheva ole tet tsere merkha final-mem
